summary: McKenzie shares that his brother's blog post went viral after being featured on Fark, overwhelming their shared VPS hosting. He spent an hour stabilizing the server configuration to handle the traffic spike. The post serves as a brief anecdote about viral content and a practical warning about server administration.
key_points: His brother's 'List of Superhero Powers' post successfully achieved viral distribution through Fark
Viral traffic can easily overwhelm a VPS, requiring emergency server administration
Version controlling Apache config files is essential insurance against breaking things during crisis fixes
money_quotes: Put your Apache config files in version control because if you bork your redirection code and don't remember how you got it working the first time you will be sorry.
key_insight: Always version control your server configuration files, because emergency changes under pressure are when you're most likely to break things irreversibly.
